stories.headline: Quezon City denies permit for third day of INC rally backing Senator Marcoleta
stories.summary: The Iglesia ni Cristo held a second day of protest on July 1 along EDSA and White Plains Avenue in Quezon City in support of Senator Rodante Marcoleta, under a city permit limiting it to White Plains Avenue from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. Police estimated about 4,000 to 4,500 participants through the day, fewer than the June 30 turnout, with roughly 2,500 officers deployed and some EDSA lanes reopened. Quezon City public order office chief Elmo San Diego said the city denied the group a permit for a planned third day, citing complaints and widespread inconvenience.
stories.context: Marcoleta, a Marcos-allied senator and INC member who briefly led the Senate Blue Ribbon Committee's flood-control corruption probe, faces a possible non-bailable plunder case over an alleged 75 million pesos in undeclared 2025 campaign donations; INC, an influential bloc-voting group, has called the case "selective justice."
stories.watch_for: The Office of the Ombudsman said on July 1 it would proceed with filing plunder charges against Marcoleta before the Sandiganbayan this week regardless of the protests.

stories.headline: AFP reports sharp rise in Chinese vessels near disputed West Philippine Sea shoals
stories.summary: The Armed Forces of the Philippines said it counted 44 Chinese coast guard and navy vessels across four West Philippine Sea features during the week of June 23-29, up from 17 the previous week. AFP spokesperson for the West Philippine Sea, Rear Admiral Roy Vincent Trinidad, said the largest concentration was near Bajo de Masinloc, with smaller numbers near Ayungin Shoal, Escoda Shoal, and the Pag-asa Islands.
stories.context: The Philippines and China have overlapping claims in the West Philippine Sea; the AFP publishes weekly vessel counts, which have ranged as high as 82 in a single week this year.

stories.headline: Belgian deputy PM visits Manila in first such trip in 33 years, eyes trade and ASEAN cooperation
stories.summary: Belgian Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Affairs Minister Maxime Prevot arrived in Manila on June 30 for a three-day visit, described by the Department of Foreign Affairs as the first by a Belgian foreign minister in 33 years, coinciding with the 80th anniversary of Belgium-Philippine relations. He met Philippine Foreign Affairs Secretary Theresa Lazaro, with both sides proposing expanded cooperation in trade, investment, maritime affairs, and labor mobility, and discussing the Philippines' 2026 ASEAN chairmanship.
stories.context: Belgium is the Philippines' 30th-largest trading partner, with bilateral trade valued at about 480 million dollars in 2025.

stories.headline: Taal Volcano logs two minor eruptions in a day; alert level unchanged
stories.summary: The Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology recorded two phreatomagmatic eruptions at Taal Volcano's main crater on June 30: the first lasted four minutes and produced a plume about 450 meters high, while a second around 2:34 p.m. lasted roughly four and a half minutes and sent ash and steam about 1,200 meters into the air before drifting southwest. Phivolcs said Taal remains under Alert Level 1, meaning low-level unrest with no mandatory evacuation required.
stories.context: Phreatomagmatic eruptions, driven by magma interacting with water, have recurred at Taal since 2021 and are considered typical behavior under Alert Level 1.

stories.headline: Tropical Depression Henry forms off Luzon; PAGASA raises then lifts Signal No. 1
stories.summary: The low-pressure area PAGASA had been tracking developed into Tropical Depression Henry, prompting the agency to raise Tropical Cyclone Wind Signal No. 1 over parts of Zambales, Bataan, and the Lubang Islands early on July 1. PAGASA lifted the signal around 11 a.m. as Henry moved away from the landmass; by mid-afternoon it was over the West Philippine Sea about 240 kilometers west-southwest of Abucay, Bataan, with peak winds of 45 kilometers per hour and gusts to 55. The agency said Henry could exit the Philippine Area of Responsibility by Wednesday evening or early Thursday.
stories.watch_for: PAGASA said Henry may intensify into a tropical storm over the West Philippine Sea by Thursday, and it issued what it called its final bulletin on the storm's combined effects with the southwest monsoon, with scattered rains still possible over parts of Luzon and the Visayas.

stories.headline: GCash parent Mynt files for record 92.3-billion-peso IPO
stories.summary: Mynt Inc., operator of e-wallet GCash, filed a registration statement with the Securities and Exchange Commission for an initial public offering of up to 8.03 billion common shares on the Philippine Stock Exchange, with an indicative maximum price of 10 pesos a share. At full size including the overallotment option, the offer could raise as much as 92.3 billion pesos, which would make it the largest IPO in Philippine history. Mynt reported 79.8 billion pesos in 2025 revenue and 17.2 billion pesos in net income, with about 39.1 million monthly active users.
stories.watch_for: The company is targeting a listing in the fourth quarter of 2026, subject to SEC review and market conditions.

stories.headline: Philippine trade deficit widens to 5.48 billion dollars in May on import surge
stories.summary: The Philippine Statistics Authority reported the trade-in-goods deficit widened to 5.48 billion dollars in May, up 50.5 percent from a year earlier and the fourth straight month of year-on-year widening, though narrower than April's revised 6.40 billion dollars. Imports rose 21.9 percent to 13.36 billion dollars while exports grew 7.6 percent to 7.87 billion dollars. An economist cited by the Inquirer attributed the gap largely to rising imports of electronics components and intermediate goods used in export production, plus a 35.6 percent jump in mineral fuel imports.
stories.context: The cumulative trade deficit for January to May 2026 reached 25.24 billion dollars, up 25 percent from the same period last year.
stories.watch_for: An economist cited in the report said the deficit could remain above 5 billion dollars through the third quarter of 2026.

stories.headline: BSP projects June inflation slowed to 6.0-7.0 percent, still above target
stories.summary: The Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas said its nowcast puts June 2026 headline inflation within a 6.0 to 7.0 percent range, compared with May's actual 6.8 percent and April's three-year-high of 7.2 percent, and still well above the central bank's 2.0-4.0 percent target band. The BSP attributed downward pressure to lower prices for rice, meat, and oil, offset by higher electricity rates and vegetable prices. The central bank also raised its full-year 2026 inflation forecast to 6.4 percent from 6.3 percent.
stories.context: The BSP's policy rate currently stands at 4.75 percent after two hikes in 2026; Governor Eli Remolona has signaled the central bank could call an off-cycle meeting if needed.
stories.watch_for: The Philippine Statistics Authority is due to release official June inflation data on July 7, 2026, with the next regular Monetary Board meeting set for August 27.

stories.headline: BPI permanently scraps fees on InstaPay and PESONet transfers
stories.summary: Bank of the Philippine Islands said it is permanently removing fees on InstaPay and PESONet fund transfers to other banks and e-wallets, effective July 1, across its app, online and mobile banking, and BanKo and BizKo platforms, with no daily cap on free InstaPay transfers. BPI previously charged 10 pesos per InstaPay transfer and 50 pesos per PESONet transfer.
stories.context: The move follows a June 17 circular from the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas lifting a nearly five-year moratorium on changes to interbank transfer fees and directing banks toward market-based pricing.

stories.headline: Globe Telecom launches Starlink direct-to-mobile satellite service after NTC approval
stories.summary: The National Telecommunications Commission approved Globe Telecom's commercial rollout of Starlink direct-to-mobile satellite connectivity, which Globe says makes it the first telecom in the Philippines, and in Southeast Asia, to offer the service commercially. It targets areas outside terrestrial mobile coverage, initially supporting voice, messaging, and data on Android LTE smartphones with active Globe SIMs and a clear view of the sky. Pricing starts at 99 pesos for prepaid and TM subscribers, while higher-tier postpaid subscribers get it free.
stories.context: Globe piloted the satellite service for disaster response after the magnitude 7.8 earthquake that struck South Cotabato, Sultan Kudarat, and Sarangani, serving more than 150,000 customers during that emergency.

stories.headline: DOLE approves largest-ever Metro Manila minimum wage hike of 85 pesos
stories.summary: The National Capital Region wage board approved an 85-peso increase to the daily minimum wage, the largest such hike the board has approved, Labor Secretary Francis Tolentino said. The non-agriculture rate rises from 695 to 780 pesos, while the rate for agriculture and small retail, service, and manufacturing firms rises from 658 to 743 pesos. DOLE estimates the hike directly benefits about 1.1 million minimum-wage earners in Metro Manila.
stories.watch_for: The increase takes effect in two tranches: 60 pesos starting July 19, 2026, and an additional 25 pesos in January 2027.

stories.headline: Iran declines direct talks with US envoys in Doha as ceasefire negotiations stall
stories.summary: Iran declined to meet US envoys Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff, who traveled to Doha, with Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei saying no meeting "at any level" with the American side was scheduled and that Tehran would deal only through Qatari and Omani mediators. Iran's chief negotiator, Mohammed Baqer Qalibaf, said sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz lies with Iran and Oman. The two sides have a 60-day window, expiring in mid-August, to reach a permanent deal after a ceasefire signed in mid-June; Iran has said it plans to impose tolls on Strait traffic if that lapses.
stories.context: The negotiations follow days of mutual strikes around the Strait of Hormuz that paused in late June; a mid-June memorandum covers frozen Iranian funds, sanctions relief on oil exports, and administration of the strait, through which about a fifth of global oil and LNG shipments pass.

stories.headline: Venezuela earthquake death toll passes 1,700 as rescue hopes fade six days on
stories.summary: The confirmed death toll from the magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 earthquakes that struck Venezuela's Caribbean coast on June 24 passed 1,700, with more than 5,000 people injured and tens of thousands still missing or unaccounted for, according to Venezuelan officials. More than 600 aftershocks have been recorded, including a magnitude 4.6 tremor on June 29. About 30,000 Venezuelan emergency workers and 2,700 foreign rescue personnel, including a French civil security regiment, are involved in search and recovery efforts, while electricity has been restored to 90 percent of La Guaira state.

stories.headline: WHO links over 1,300 excess deaths in Europe to ongoing record heatwave
stories.summary: The World Health Organization said more than 1,300 excess deaths across Europe have been linked to a heatwave underway since June 21, Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said. Record temperatures were recorded in Slovakia (41 degrees Celsius), Czechia (41.9 degrees, breaking its prior national record), and Hungary (41.8 degrees), with at least 130 million people across Central and Eastern Europe experiencing temperatures above 35 degrees Celsius on June 29. Red heat warnings were issued for 22 Italian cities, while wildfires hit Croatia, Albania, and Bosnia and Herzegovina.
stories.context: Tedros said Europe is warming at roughly twice the global average, making it the fastest-warming continent.
stories.watch_for: Forecasters expect another heat surge from July 5 affecting France, Spain, Germany, Italy, Switzerland, and parts of the UK.

stories.headline: Ukraine strikes Russian refineries as overnight attacks kill three; Kyiv signs Gripen jet deal
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Ukraine struck an oil refinery in Ufa and a military-industrial facility in Russia's Penza region, calling it a "just response" in its campaign against Russian energy and defense sites. Overnight Russian drone attacks killed at least three people, in the Dnipropetrovsk region and Zaporizhzhia, according to local officials and Ukrainian media. Kyiv also signed a contract with Sweden for 16 used Gripen E fighter jets worth about 2.53 billion dollars, with deliveries from 2027.
stories.context: Ukraine has intensified strikes on Russian oil refineries, contributing to fuel shortages that President Vladimir Putin has publicly acknowledged; Moscow continues to demand that Ukraine cede the Donetsk, Luhansk, Kherson, and Zaporizhia regions.

stories.headline: House prosecutors weigh seeking lower Senate threshold to convict VP Duterte before July 6 trial
stories.summary: An adviser to the House prosecution team, former lawmaker Robert Ace Barbers, said prosecutors may ask the Senate impeachment court to lower the number of votes needed to convict Vice President Sara Duterte, arguing that some senators may be unable to take part in the trial. Conviction currently requires two-thirds of the Senate, or 16 of 24 members, under the Constitution. Barbers said the matter would be raised "at the appropriate time." The trial is set to begin July 6.
stories.context: Duterte faces an impeachment trial on articles that include alleged unexplained wealth, misuse of confidential funds, and threats against President Marcos and his family; a conviction would remove her from office.
stories.watch_for: The Senate impeachment court is scheduled to begin the trial on July 6, 2026.

stories.headline: Marcos begins four-day official visit to Canada to pursue trade and security deals
stories.summary: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. left for Vancouver on July 1 for a four-day official visit to Canada, the first by a Philippine president in 11 years, at the invitation of Prime Minister Mark Carney. Palace Press Officer Claire Castro said the two governments were expected to sign agreements on energy and natural resources, trade and investment, labor and immigration, and tourism and the arts, and that Marcos would advance talks on a free trade agreement. Presidential Communications Office official Dave Gomez said the trip would proceed despite the INC protests in Metro Manila.
stories.context: The Philippines holds the 2026 ASEAN chairmanship and has been seeking to broaden its trade and security partnerships beyond traditional allies.
stories.watch_for: The visit runs through July 4, 2026, and includes meetings with Prime Minister Carney and Canadian business stakeholders in mining, telecommunications, and other sectors.

stories.headline: Senate opens inquiry into Tacloban school shooting and online violence
stories.summary: A Senate committee led by Senator Risa Hontiveros opened an inquiry on July 1 into the shooting at San Jose National High School in Tacloban City and possible links between online gaming platforms and violence among young people. The panel invited relatives of the slain students, some attending in person and others remotely, along with agencies including the Department of Social Welfare and Development. Several other officials sent representatives.
stories.context: The June 22 shooting at the Tacloban school killed three students, and two minors were taken into custody. Authorities have linked the case to online communities they say expose young people to extremist and violent content.

stories.headline: CIDG recommends homicide charges over deaths of two Ateneo basketball players
stories.summary: The Department of Justice said it received a supplemental referral from the Criminal Investigation and Detection Group on July 1 recommending charges of homicide, or reckless imprudence resulting in homicide, over the deaths of two Ateneo de Manila basketball players. The referral supplements a June 26 recommendation that had sought complaints under the Anti-Hazing Act against former head coach Tab Baldwin and 11 others, including team staff. The DOJ said both referrals remain under evaluation.
stories.context: The Criminal Investigation and Detection Group's earlier referral cited Republic Act No. 11053, the Anti-Hazing Act, in connection with the deaths tied to the Ateneo Blue Eagles basketball program.

stories.headline: Marcos weighs extending price ceiling on imported rice
stories.summary: President Marcos was studying whether to extend a 50-peso-per-kilo price ceiling on imported rice after the National Price Coordinating Council recommended keeping it, Palace Press Officer Claire Castro said, adding a decision could come within the day before Marcos left for Canada. The cap on 5-percent broken imported rice was first imposed for 30 days under Executive Order No. 118 on May 13 to curb what the government called unreasonable price increases. Rice imports reached 2.31 million metric tons from January to May, up about 20 percent from a year earlier.
stories.watch_for: Castro said a decision on whether to extend the price ceiling could be announced within the day.

stories.headline: Six Philippine esports teams qualify for Asian Games; PUBG Mobile squad falls short
stories.summary: Six national esports teams under the Philippines' Sibol program qualified for the Asian Games in Nagoya, competing in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang, Honor of Kings, Naraka: Bladepoint, Puyo Puyo Champions, Pokemon UNITE, and Identity V, the Philippine Esports Organization said. The PUBG Mobile team narrowly missed qualifying, losing to Jordan by a single point on a tiebreaker. PESO executive director Marlon Marcelo said the federation hoped for at least a medal or two.

stories.headline: South Korea's monthly exports top 100 billion dollars for first time on chip demand
stories.summary: South Korea's exports exceeded 100 billion dollars in a single month for the first time in June, according to government figures released July 1, driven by record semiconductor shipments amid strong global demand from the artificial intelligence sector. Chipmakers SK Hynix and Samsung Electronics were cited as the main drivers of the record.
